title: Dashboard SSE resilience: Last-Event-ID reconnect + auth + server ring buffer (Rec#9)
description: Make the Dashboard SSE connection resilient to disconnections and authenticated: (1) replace bare EventSource with a fetch-based SSE client that can send Authorization header and track Last-Event-ID, (2) on reconnect pass the last received event ID so the server can replay missed events, (3) add a small in-memory ring buffer (e.g. last 50 events) to the server events.ts endpoint for reconnect replay. Eliminates silent event loss on network drops.

changes: Replace bare EventSource with fetch-based SSE or @microsoft/fetch-event-source (or similar)
Track lastEventId in a ref; pass as Last-Event-ID header or ?lastEventId= query param on reconnect
Include Authorization: Bearer TOKEN header on connection (read token from config/env)
Implement reconnect with exponential backoff on connection error
Add ring buffer (circular array, capacity 50 events) storing { id, data, type, timestamp }
On new SSE connection: check Last-Event-ID header or ?lastEventId query param
If Last-Event-ID present: replay all buffered events with id > lastEventId before streaming new events
Assign monotonically increasing numeric IDs to all emitted events (already done per audit; confirm client-visible)
Existing SSE connection limit (10) and event generation logic unchanged

implementation: Read packages/dashboard/src/hooks/use-events.ts to understand current EventSource usage and event handling
Read packages/server/src/api/events.ts to understand event ID generation, existing SSE setup, and connection management
Check existing dashboard dependencies for fetch-event-source or similar; add @microsoft/fetch-event-source if absent
Refactor use-events.ts: replace EventSource constructor with fetchEventSource call; add Authorization header; track lastEventId in useRef
Add ring buffer to events.ts: simple circular array with push/replay methods; wire into event emission path
Add Last-Event-ID / lastEventId query param handling in events.ts connection handler
Test: disconnect dashboard, trigger 5 events server-side, reconnect — confirm 5 events replayed

reference.pattern: @microsoft/fetch-event-source or EventSource polyfill with auth header support
reference.examples: Check existing package.json for fetch-event-source; if absent prefer @microsoft/fetch-event-source (MIT, widely used)

rationale.chosen_approach: fetch-based SSE client + server ring buffer — standard approach for authenticated SSE with replay
alternatives_considered: WebSocket upgrade (overkill for unidirectional server→client events; SSE is simpler)
query-param token only (works but Authorization header is more standard and doesn't expose token in server logs)
decision_factors: Native EventSource cannot send custom headers — fetch-based SSE is the canonical workaround
Server ring buffer requires minimal memory (50 events × ~1KB = ~50KB max) and no persistence
rationale.tradeoffs: Fetch-based SSE may behave differently in some browser edge cases vs native EventSource; covered by testing

risks.description: fetch-event-source library may not be in current dependency tree; adding it increases bundle size
risks.mitigation: Check existing deps first; @microsoft/fetch-event-source is ~4KB gzipped; alternatively implement minimal custom fetch-SSE reader (~20 lines)
risks.fallback: Use query-param token (?token=xxx) with native EventSource as fallback if no fetch-SSE lib available

risks.description: Ring buffer replay may send duplicate events if client receives events before disconnect is detected
risks.mitigation: Client deduplicates by event ID; ring buffer replay only sends events strictly after lastEventId
risks.fallback: Accept small duplicate window; dashboard UI should be idempotent for event display

criteria: Dashboard SSE connection sends Authorization header (verifiable in browser network tab)
After reconnect, events generated during disconnect are received by dashboard (ring buffer replay works)
Last-Event-ID is tracked client-side and sent on reconnect
Server ring buffer holds last 50 events (not more, not zero)
Existing dashboard event display functionality unchanged
pnpm build --filter dashboard produces no TypeScript errors
convergence.verification: Open dashboard, disconnect network for 10s (trigger events server-side), reconnect — confirm missed events appear; inspect network tab and confirm Authorization header present on SSE request
convergence.definition_of_done: Dashboard does not silently lose events during brief network disconnections; SSE connection is authenticated at the same level as REST API calls

unit: Ring buffer: pushing 51 events drops oldest, keeps newest 50
Ring buffer: replayFrom(id) returns events with id > given id in insertion order
use-events hook: lastEventId ref updates on each received event
integration: SSE reconnect with Last-Event-ID: server replays buffered events since that ID
SSE connection without auth token when FABRIC_AUTH_TOKEN set: returns 401
manual_checks: Browser network inspector: SSE request shows Authorization header
Disconnect/reconnect and confirm missed events appear in dashboard event log
success_metrics: 0 events lost during reconnect for events in the last 50-event ring buffer window
SSE auth header present on 100% of dashboard SSE connections

commit.message_template: feat(dashboard): SSE resilience — Last-Event-ID reconnect, auth header, server ring buffer replay
